title: Urophyllum canthiiflorum
intro: Urophyllum canthiiflorum, also known as the "Bengal Urophyllum", is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Urophyllum canthiiflorum include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and skin creams, to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Urophyllum canthiiflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is part of the genus Urophyllum, which includes around 80 species of flowering plants. The Rubiaceae family, also known as the madder family, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 13,000 species. Urophyllum canthiiflorum is native to the tropical regions of Asia. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.
